mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals imminent discount-rate relief for equities, so S&P 500 prices should rise over the next month when policy is being eased. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because lower short rates then translate into lower real financing costs and higher valuation multiples.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are negative or below baseline in periods where FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if the sign is positive during repeated tightening periods with no loss of predictive accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals labor-market deterioration and weaker future household income, which should depress risk appetite and S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is intended to capture a demand-slowdown transition rather than a steady high-unemployment state.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal disappears once PAYEMS is flat or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad domestic demand weakening, which should lower cyclicals and the index over the following quarter. The mechanism depends on both consumption and housing softening together; either alone is not enough.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if either series alone explains the effect as well as the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO while GDP growth is firm indicates expanding real activity and improving corporate revenue prospects, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next month. This is a pro-cyclical signal that works when industrial production is accelerating rather than merely stable.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.4% month-over-month and GDPC1 is positive year-over-year
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not generate positive 20-day SPX returns above the unconditional mean, or if the effect vanishes when GDP growth is already negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy becomes tighter and valuation multiples should compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The mechanism is a short-horizon repricing of discount rates rather than a growth effect.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL accelerates for 2 consecutive months and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered combination is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if inflation acceleration alone without high FEDFUNDS produces the same effect.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS implies payroll growth is slowing, which should weaken expected earnings and reduce equity prices over the next quarter. This is most informative when unemployment has not yet surged, because the market reacts to the early cooling signal before recession is obvious.
failure_condition: Reject if falling PAYEMS is not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive once UNRATE is already rising sharply.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand rebound in both GDPC1 and RSAFS should lift sales expectations and support equity returns over the next month. The mechanism requires concurrent improvement in output and spending; isolated gains in one series should not be enough to generate the signal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both accelerate versus their prior release
failure_condition: Reject if joint acceleration in GDP and retail sales does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST signals housing-cycle strength and stronger downstream demand for materials, labor, and consumer durables, which should lift the S&P 500 over the next quarter. The effect is expected when housing starts are moving up from a subdued base, not when they are already near cycle peaks.
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST increases are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses when industrial production is weakening.
